title: Visual search query generation
abstract: A system for generating a search query is disclosed, which includes a processor and a computer-readable medium storing instructions that controls the system to perform rendering a GUI for an application, which includes a first visual object associated with a first operation; in response to detecting that a user has selected the first visual object during a first mode of operation, executing the first operation; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first visual object during a second mode of operation, performing disabling the first operation; inferring, from information related to the first visual object, one or more search filtering options contextually relevant to the first visual object; displaying, via the GUI; and in response to detecting that the user has selected one of the search filtering options, adding the selected search filtering option to a search query.
claims:
Claims What is claimed is: 1. A system for generating a search query, comprising: a processor; and a computer-readable medium in communication with the processor and storing instructions that, when executed by the processor, cause the processor to control the system to perform: rendering a graphical user interface, GUI, for an application, the GUI comprising a plurality of visual objects including a first visual object associated with a first operation; in response to detecting that a user has selected the first visual object during a first mode of operation, executing the first operation associated with the first visual object; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first visual object during a second mode of operation, performing: disabling the first operation associated with the first visual object; inferring, from information related to the first visual object, one or more search filtering options contextually relevant to the first visual object; displaying, via the GUI, the one or more search filtering options including a first search filtering option; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first search filtering option, adding the selected first search filtering option to a search query; wherein the one or more search filtering options are displayed adjacent to, at least partially overlapping or in a position corresponding to the first visual object. 2. The system of claim 1, wherein, for inferring the one or more search filtering options, the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to control the system to perform: searching a data collection to identify one or more data entities contextually relevant to the first visual object; and determining, based on the one or more identified data entities, the one or more search filtering options. 3. The system of claim 2, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to control the system to perform: in response to receiving a user input requesting to execute a search, searching the data collection to retrieve one or more data entities relevant to the search query; and displaying, via the GUI, the one or more retrieved data entities as a search result for the search query. 4. The system of any preceding claim, wherein: the plurality of visual objects further includes a second visual object associated with a second operation, and the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to control the system to perform: in response to detecting that the user has selected the second visual object during the first mode of operation, executing the second operation associated with the second visual object; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first visual object during the second mode of operation, performing: disabling the second operation associated with the second visual object; inferring, based on information related to the second visual object, one or more search filtering options contextually relevant to the second visual object; displaying, via the GUI, the one or more search filtering options including a second search filtering option; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the second search filtering option, adding the selected second search filtering option to the search query. 5. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to control the system to perform displaying the search query on the GUI. 6. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the instructions, when executed by the processor, further cause the processor to control the system to perform: receiving, during the second mode of operation, a user text input including a search term; and adding the received search term to the search query. 7. The system of any preceding claim, wherein the system comprises an artificial intelligence, Al, engine and/or deep-leaming, DL, engine, and the system is configured to use the Al engine and/or DL engine to provide more accurate and relevant estimation of a search filtering option, based upon user selection history provided to the Al engine and/or DL engine. 8. A method of operating a system for generating a search query, comprising: rendering a graphical user interface GUI for an application, the GUI comprising a plurality of visual objects including a first visual object associated with a first operation; in response to detecting that a user has selected the first visual object during a first mode of operation, executing the first operation associated with the first visual object; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first visual object during a second mode of operation, performing: disabling the first operation associated with the first visual object; inferring, from information related to the first visual object, one or more search filtering options contextually relevant to the first visual object; displaying, via the GUI, the one or more search filtering options including a first search filtering option; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first search filtering option, adding the selected first search filtering option to a search query wherein the one or more search filtering options are displayed adjacent to, at least partially overlapping or in a position corresponding to the first visual object. 9. The method of claim 8, wherein inferring the one or more search filtering options comprises: searching a data collection associated with the application to identify one or more data entities contextually relevant to the first visual object; and 

 determining, based on the one or more identified data entities, the one or more search filtering options. 10. The method of claim 9, further comprising: in response to receiving a user input requesting to execute a search, searching the data collection to retrieve one or more data entities relevant to the search query; and displaying, via the GUI, the one or more retrieved data entities as a search result for the search query. 11. The method of any of claims 8 to 10, wherein: the plurality of visual objects further includes a second visual object associated with a second operation, and the method further comprises: in response to detecting that the user has selected the second visual object during the first mode of operation, executing the second operation associated with the second visual object; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the first visual object during the second mode of operation, performing: disabling the second operation associated with the second visual object; inferring, based on information related to the second visual object, one or more search filtering options contextually relevant to the second visual object; displaying, via the GUI, the one or more search filtering options including a second search filtering option; and in response to detecting that the user has selected the second search filtering option, adding the selected second search filtering option to the search query. 12. The method of any of claims 8 to 11, further comprising displaying the search query on the GUI. 13. The method of any of claims 8 to 12, further comprising: 22 

 receiving, during the second mode of operation, a user text input comprising a search term; and adding the received search term to the search query. 14. The method of any preceding claim, further comprising using an artificial intelligence, Al, engine and/or deep-leaming, DL, engine to provide more accurate and relevant estimation of a search filtering option, based upon user selection history provided to the Al engine and/or DL engine. 15. A non-transitory computer-readable medium storing executable instructions that, when executed by a processor, cause the processor to carry out the method of any one of claims 8 to 14. 23
